reviews.review_msg: I feel like he overcomplicated a relatively simple course which made the experience kind of terrible.
GPA killer, easy concepts taught in class but hard exams, he is a hearless prof that doesnt care about our marks. Hope you can find a better prof and good luck everyone

Dr. Pokrishevsky was fantastic, his lectures were very interesting and he was always available outside of class. He was happy to answer all of my questions and was always kind to students. His exams were not too difficult, and the quizzes helped keep me up to date with the material.
Eddie is a great prof. Loved the specimens he would bring to class. His lectures are engaging and he is always willing to help students.
Eddie is excellent. Lectures were very engaging and fun, and the course was very well structures. You can tell he cares about his students.

Prof Pokrishevsky was a great lecturer - he truly cares for his students, and has always found the time to meet with me outside of class and office hours to answer questions. He also made the course quite enjoyable by bringing in various specimens to class and giving us extra resources. Exam were not easy, but fair. A solid choice for BISC300.
BISC300 was a very interesting course, and Eddie was a great teacher. He is passionate, and you can tell that he cares about his students. Lectures were engaging and fun.

I do NOT recommend him to anyone. His lectures are informative, but he goes really fast so it's hard to catch onto the concepts. Whenever I asked him a question, it seemed like he didn't want to answer. He makes you seem dumb for not knowing the answer, then proceeds to give a horrible explanation acting like the answer is already obvious.
Two words: GPA Killer

Very interesting course, and the lecturers were excellent. I was looking forward to attend every lecture. Exams were based on the material learned in class - make sure you study his presentations and focus on the learning objectives.

While the course content was interesting this prof did not seem to care about the low class averages, as he did not scale the final grades. Even the exams and quizzes were quite a mess with confusing wording and that made it quite difficult to correctly answer the questions. Overall, I would not recommend this prof when taking Bisc 300.
I feel like he overcomplicated a relatively simple course which made the experience kind of terrible.
GPA killer, easy concepts taught in class but hard exams, he is a hearless prof that doesnt care about our marks. Hope you can find a better prof and good luck everyone

Dr. Pokrishevsky was fantastic, his lectures were very interesting and he was always available outside of class. He was happy to answer all of my questions and was always kind to students. His exams were not too difficult, and the quizzes helped keep me up to date with the material.
Eddie is a great prof. Loved the specimens he would bring to class. His lectures are engaging and he is always willing to help students.
Eddie is excellent. Lectures were very engaging and fun, and the course was very well structures. You can tell he cares about his students.

Prof Pokrishevsky was a great lecturer - he truly cares for his students, and has always found the time to meet with me outside of class and office hours to answer questions. He also made the course quite enjoyable by bringing in various specimens to class and giving us extra resources. Exam were not easy, but fair. A solid choice for BISC300.
BISC300 was a very interesting course, and Eddie was a great teacher. He is passionate, and you can tell that he cares about his students. Lectures were engaging and fun.

I do NOT recommend him to anyone. His lectures are informative, but he goes really fast so it's hard to catch onto the concepts. Whenever I asked him a question, it seemed like he didn't want to answer. He makes you seem dumb for not knowing the answer, then proceeds to give a horrible explanation acting like the answer is already obvious.
Two words: GPA Killer

Very interesting course, and the lecturers were excellent. I was looking forward to attend every lecture. Exams were based on the material learned in class - make sure you study his presentations and focus on the learning objectives.

While the course content was interesting this prof did not seem to care about the low class averages, as he did not scale the final grades. Even the exams and quizzes were quite a mess with confusing wording and that made it quite difficult to correctly answer the questions. Overall, I would not recommend this prof when taking Bisc 300.

Arne is passionate about what he teaches, so he really makes you think and expects you to know the material/textbook inside out. Weekly quizzes were a bit too specific, but not horrible. Lecture material is alright, only thing is he speaks too fast and you need to show up to class. NEED to understand the textbook topics in order to do well.

Under no circumstances take courses with it, no office hours, insane reading, brutally difficult exams no one did well in, inaccurate learning objectives,rude when asked questions, NOT clear grading criteria since he cranked up the grading scale to 95+ AFTER he released the grades. This man expects way to much for his poor teaching and attitude.

Dr. Mooers is an excellent professor; lectures are engaging and it is clear that he is passionate about the class topics. Exams are difficult but not unreasonable. The class requires lots of studying with weekly quizzes, 3 exams, and a few projects, but overall is very interesting, and Dr. Mooers does a good job of explaining concepts.

Sometimes people should just stick to research.... His course takes half the workload of the semester and his exams are out of the world, like truly out of the world. There is absolutely no way you can do good on them. Also there is no scaling. It's sad that his course is a monopoly. DO NOT TAKE WITH HIM

Rude and condescending when you ask him questions as if he gets offended. The course takes up half your workload and he does not have office hours. Lots of readings that don't help with the understanding. Material is all over the place and no source of practice or homework. Avoid at all costs

Lectures were kind of dull but they weren't recorded so you really had to pay attention and he kind of mumbles and trails off halfway through sentences. The textbook also doesn't really help if you're trying to understand concepts. His test questions are also really hard to understand. Also weekly quizzes... good luck.

3 midterms, weekly quizzes (7 total), 2 projects, each lecture had 60+ slides and this class covered 2+ textbook chapters weekly. The prof changed the last midterm date so he could extend a trip - the alternative was a cumulative exam during exam week. He's nice, passionate, and has interesting classes but this class is a time monopolizer.

Weekly quizzes, 4 projects, 3 midterms (no final). Insane work load. No office hours. Test avg always a fail & doesn't scale. Threw everything at the one TA for marking, & when he finally marked one test Q, his 11 y.o. daughter marked it wrong & the TA had to fix all the exams. Completely useless prof. Very rude/condescending when u ask for help

This class was awful. Arne had so many assignments due worth 5-10% each but took way too much work, some assignments were just weird and useless, and he was honestly not the nicest person. He could be condescending when you ask simple questions and spoke like you're at a bloody auction (too fast!). 3 Midterms were 10% each and ridiculously hard :(
Awesome evolution prof. Tests could be tricky but grading was lax.

Arne is very passionate about the topics he teaches. I would recommend listening to the lectures since he talks fast. The midterms were conceptual and difficult. They were also very lengthy. The group work and quizzes were fair.

Dr. Mooers is definitely passionate about what he does. If you want to learn about how vast and interesting the field of evolutionary biology is, take BISC 300 with him - he includes lots of current research into his lectures. My only real criticism - he talks very fast and maybe tries to incorporate too much information into each lecture.

Very interesting lectures, talks fast but lectures are recorded so you can replay if needed. Great prof personality wise, he's not an expert in all topics covered in the course though. 3 Midterms and no final. One quiz every week. One group project in tutorial involving a presentation and creation of a podcast, would take his class again. :)

Okay so he's a decent professor but I wouldn't give him a 4.4 rating I'd say more like 3.6. I've definitley seen more dedicated and passionate profs, not that he isn't but in comparisson he is basically just a regular professor. I advise you ask for lots of help and go into more detail than he asks for when using formulas and you'll be fine.
Fair professor. Fair exams. However,I did not like that the final had fifteen marks dedicated to a lecture that he barely taught us two days prior.

3 midterms, 1 project(with presentation), and no final. However, his exams are EXTREMELY hard. Thank god we had awesome TA's who marked leniently. He speaks really fast in class so you have to pay attention. Tons of material to learn (2-3 chapters a week) + class notes. He makes things interesting, but I think gives way too much work.

Really good prof, talks pretty fast so try to convince him to record his lectures (helps to go back and review) he is really flexible about course format (we had 3 midterms and 3 assignments and no final) his tests were really conceptual and hard but hey, its not first year anymore kids! anyway if your serious about science take his courses.
Super interesting lecturer. His notes are ok but they often need to be supplemented with material from the textbook. His assignments (3) are challenging but his exams are fair.
I loved his lectures. He really inspires one to learn and be successful. Highly recommended for all the courses he teaches. He knows a lot of stuff other than just science/biology. A very smart guy.

awesome prof! had high interest in the subject and made sure you did too by the end of the semester. seriously learned lots and enjoyed the classwork. exams are tough but scaled. presentations also boosted course grade quite a bit. recommended!!

AWESOME.  Great lecturer, very helpful.  He actually learned most of the class's names!  I couldn't believe it.
 
 Loves his field, and makes you love it too.  Not too difficult.  Average was a C+/B-.

Super sweet and very willing to answer questions. A lot of the quizzes were very picky, and there was way too much material on the second midterm. Slides can be a little confusing and she's not the most emotive speaker but she's okay.

Nicole is amazing. Had her for BISC204 as well. Never any surprises on her exams and doesn't just test memorized facts, she wants you to apply it to other situations. Didn't use the textbook at all. Just her notes are enough to get you through with a good grade.

Dr. Tunbridge is really enthusiastic about the material and genuinely interested in helping students - answered a ton of my questions on a late night right before the exam! One of the things I really appreciated was that she references classic papers from literature and also keeps students informed of volunteering opportunities in the department.

I might get flamed for this but I thought the course was decent. My big advice was to read the textbook!!! I barely studied his lecture notes and did well on the exams. I recommend understanding concepts plus memorizing since he asked both types of questions on the tests. Evolution has some very abstract concepts so do not cram last minute.

This is the worst class with the worst prof I have ever taken. Exams, assignments, and tutorials are not coordinated at all. No one is of any help and all they tell you is to read the textbook. I do not understand how he was chosen to be a prof for this class

I don't believe in bad mouthing a professor unless they are horrendous, so I will instead give advice. Read the textbook religiously, page to page. Practice the supplementary "problems" he provides with no end. Force him in class (by asking questions in advance) to keep the class going, as it often repeats. Or do not take this class.

This was probably the worst class I have ever taken. The course material was pretty straightforward, however the exams were totally left field and were more theory based. On top of that, his teaching wasn't the greatest, and tutorials and assignments did not correlate. I totally regret taking the course with him, try to take it with someone else...

Overall he is a "fair" prof, not the best, not the worst. His questions made you apply concepts learned, which are excellent, if only he actually taught the concepts well. Heavy use of textbook readings, and class/tutorials do little to explain the readings in a better manner. Overall, good guy, bad class.

While I think that Alex was actually good at explaining concepts, he was AWFUL at writing questions. No idea what he wanted half the time. And just telling people to read an entire textbook was not helpful at all. TA's also didn't help much. He's a nice guy though and has a good sense of humour.

People really seemed to dislike him, but I've had FAR worse profs in university. He tried to make things interesting. I rather liked his class. His class has more applied questions vs. pure memorization which a lot of people HIGHLY disliked, but it seemed to work for me. Hated that he gave a 2 pg research essay worth 3% like one week before finals.

He is a nice guy, but unfortunately didn't take the time to structure the class. The notes have little to do with the textbook and the textbook has little to do with the class. All his questions were really open ended and marked extremely hard. T.A's could have been replaced with a Santa decoration: useless. Unable to answer basic questions. AVOID!

Nice guy, bad prof. Exams are all application type questions, but his lectures are summaries of the text and mostly conceptual. Isn't clear with what he wants, and not entirely fair with marking. I didn't try as hard in this class as I should have, but he was no help. I'll probably end up retaking with a better prof.

He is bad at explaining and his questions are confusing. He does not communicate with his TAs so they have no idea what is going on. When you ask for clarification of questions they don't even understand the questions he makes up himself. Notes are completely useless, filled with pictures and doesn't cover main concepts from the book.
As Frank Costanza once said, "This guy... this is not my kind of guy."

Alex really knows his stuff and he seems really passionate about the material. Unfortunately, he's not a good prof. The tutorials and lectures did little to prepare me for exams. I felt like I was studying in the dark when it came to exam time. The tutorials were pointless and lectures tended to be rather vague. Try to avoid this prof if possible

Alex is a great guy; He is very approachable, and acts kindly to his students. Sadly, his course is questionable at best. In example, one entire tutorial (50min) was spent explaining Phylogenetic tree formation; we were never examined on this. You'll spend hours reading, and class won't ever help to explain what you read before hand. Do not take.

Quick list of 'problem areas': Under explained concepts. Over explained details. Baren class notes. Misleading exam questions. Little feedback via marking (doesn't explain mark distribution). Poor use of time in tutorials. Last minute or poorly thought out assignments. Exhaustive use of textbook. Intended target audience: PhD students.

There is only one problem with this prof. It is not that his class is extremely watered down. Its not that his exams are very concentrated. Its not that his tutorials are pointless. It's that he's a brand new professor. Sure he should have had a year of PDP, but it seems like his target audience is PhD, not 2nd/3rd year students.

Nice guy & obviously really smart, but he doesn't explain things well & seems to forget we're not all as smart as he is. Needs to be able to simplify his explanations and be a little more clear. Wouldn't recommend taking a class with him right now, but he does have potential to be a great prof. Threw a lot of entertaining material in there for us.

Would not recommend. His lecture notes are very broad, exams are very detailed. He says read the textbook, but most of the textbook is material that is irrelevant to the course material. Tutorials were useless. I had a great TA, but we did nothing except for quizzes and read papers when we could've been going over concepts. No exam review, nothing.
Should have been an online course, all he does is tell you to go read the book. Not that it helps..

When it comes down to it, he can't teach. He doesn't go into ANY kind of detail and just expects us to understand everything hes not saying. The textbook is useless and unrelated. Both the texts and lectures are filled with information we're not tested on and the information we do need is no where to be found. 50% average is his fault, NOT OURS.

He's a pretty bad prof. His lectures are fairly round-about. He tends to skim over topics vaguely, but then expects very specific answers on exams. The average for both midterm exams were right around 50%. Honestly, the best part of this course was filling out the prof evaluation forms.

He is the worst professor I have had yet at SFU. Lectures are not informative and his exams are not fair at all. The only advice he ever gives is read the textbook. I have been reading the textbook and his exam is not relevant. He should reflect on the fact that the class average is so low, and that it is likely not the students' fault but his own.

hate to say this cause he's such a nice guy and really knowledgeable about the course.. but he is awful at teaching. Quiz and exam questions were vague and unclear and he expected very specific and detailed answers. The average for both midterms was around 50, which is extremely low for bisc300.

I'm not here to bad mouth the guy, but he was a pretty awful prof. He does seem like a generally nice guy, but not much of a teacher. Purple-monkey-dishwasher (for any Simpsons fans who need cheering up :)

Need more ability to explain the concepts. USELESS TUTORIALS the only good part was to have weekly quizzes at the beginning of tutorials rest of it was a waste of time. Should have discussed and solved questions relevant to the exams in the tutorial. Also,one of the TAs didn't know the course well.

"A naive professor" can best describe Chubaty. He has little experience with teaching. Class time was spent explaining pictures rather than concepts. Tutorial time was spent vaguely explaining concepts rather than applying them. Midterms were somewhat fair. He gave no indication as to what you got right or wrong on each question (Arbitrary mark)

He is a brand new 'sessional' professor and it shows. Class is concept based whereas the exams and assignments were applications of the concepts. There was no previous practice with applying concepts as class ended up being a textbook summary and tutorials ended up being largely useless. AVOID until the class structure improves.

Overall he can clearly talk and you can see he enjoys it. Unfortunately he cannot structure the class. Assignments were paced randomly. Midterms were ridiculous and he uses an excuse to say students dont answer questions to say that an avg was over 50 instead of saying it was way below because it was unfair. Ridiculous 10 markQ on 32 or 36 mark MT

If you are a mind reader, then this class should be easy. Then again, if you're a mind reader, any class should be easy. The prof is extremely vague and the tutorials were just a way to keep students in one place an hour a week. Try to avoid this prof if possible, or be a psychic. Either or

Chubaty has to potential teach. However the way class is structured is very poor. The class is a summary of the textbook. Tutorials do not apply concepts, but rather recite them. You should be dedicated to reading the book and studying attentively, otherwise this class will become overwhelming very quickly.

If you could avoid this prof, do it! He is terrible at explaining. His lectures are useless and very brief. His exams are harshly marked and the questions are extremely ambiguous. You just can't study for them cause he doesn't say what he expects us to know. One exam question was a random graph. His TA's were also useless. Worst course ever!

Takes forever to get to the point and sometimes doesn't even get to the point. His lecture slides are all pictures and he just talks in round about explanations. Midterm was very hard considering the average was ~50% for a bio class. Gave an assignment 5 days before midterm 1, now giving another assignment week of midterm 2.

His lectures are educational and entertaining, but expects students to "read his mind" about what he wants as answers on assignments -- if you want a diagram, SAY SO. The midterm was not representative of the material emphasized in class, tutorials, or even the midterm review.

It's really hard to judge what could be on his exams. Sometimes an obscure experiment from the textbook ends up being a 4 mark question on an exam that's out of 32 marks. Things he emphasized didn't make it onto the midterm yet things we barely covered were on there. Not a bad guy though, and he tries to make lectures interesting
He's pretty cute and he makes class interesting.

Very tough professor. He's confusing and it's like he purposely tries to trick you. Tells you to focus on the broader concept but then quizzes you on super specific material. I think there are only 2 profs that teach bisc 300 and he's better than the other guy so you really have no choice. 5 quizzes50% of final grade, no midterm. Hard tests. GL.
I got a D because he can't read my writing. When I went to his office he was very mean. What a jerk.

A brilliant and prestigious professor. Crespi teaches for the sake of you actually learning things, NOT purely for you to pass some exam. As a result his exams might seem weird and difficult. But going through with his class really makes you learn stuff, not just because you have to. Great sense of humor, highly recommended.

Class-entertaining but hated those demonic TA's.Test were hard, somewhat manageable on top of dat hard marking by TA's made it worse.If this keeps up there won't be that many student's left in BS,Dear.The skinny TA is dillusional,seems friendly but looks for a specfic answer on hypothetical questions which can be answered in many different ways.
Good course, learned a lot. Overall would take again.

Spent too much time on assignments in class (ended up being a month behind in material). Mumbled and was very unclear. Group work on exams took away from time on individual mark which took away from my grade overall. Tutorial mark was annoying to receive (not based on attendance only). Didnt fix mic when talking so there was constant feedback.

Mika was a great person and was accommodating during online classes due to COVID; however, I found his lecturing poor as it sounded like he was just going off a script. I also thought the assignments given in this class were unnecessary and didn't give proper opportunity to show what you learned. Tests were fair. Good person, poorly designed class.

Although lecture slides weren't always the most clear, lectures themselves were decent and exams were fair. Mika does a good job of explaining concepts and pointing out particularly important information, and the course content itself is quite interesting. Definitely a solid prof.

He doesnt seem to know what hes talking about. He reads off the lecture slides (which I doubt was made by him) and he still has a hard time getting the main points across. It feels like a high school kid is attempting to teach a university level course by skimming through the textbook once. Do not recommend this prof.
Pretty clear as far as lecturers go, and the slides were more or less self explanatory. The quizzes were okay, if a little picky. His final straight from the slides. Nice guy.

Worst professor ever. You may find the lectures easy and they are, however when it comes to exam time she focuses on tiny detail that she did not cover for more than 5 seconds. She does not record her lectures and does not use the microphone, so they are mandatory to attend. She is unorganized and puts lectures up very late.
Very good prof. Interested in the subject and very lively at lectures. Always happy to see student during office hours. Can explain details very well and answer questions very well.

GREAT prof! she knows her stuff and can communicate that knowledge in lectures really well. midterm was easy if you study her notes and refer to txtbook if anything unclear. and great attitude to studens! had her for bisc102 lab, she was good instructor for that too
awesome prof
Relatively unprepared for lecture.  Extremely boring.  Tends to ask ridiculously hard questions on exams.  Definately hit up Renaissance before lecture.

Clear, extremely helpful when asked (staying well beyond his hours) - and worked hard to be entertaining in Lecture.  Good sense of humour. He helped me recover from an F on the first midterm to a B+ in the course.  One of the top BIO teachers I have encountered, though he doesn't give grades for free. Great teaching - has my respect and gratitude.

really funny, interesting..sometimes his tests can be tricky because there aren't much examples in the lecture nots to go by, but he will do anything that he can in order to help you do will in the class. I got a C+ on the first midterm, but recovered for an A in the class at the end....very helpful

I've learned alot from this class.  I would name him at his origin as a "pretty interesting guy"...then he evolved into a "pretty boring, rambling bloke"...I JUST wish he went extinct at the end, or perhaps he shouldve been selected against, or maybe someone should've given him adrenaline shots at the beginning of each class.
Rolf needs to recognize that we're in 2025, not 2000. Students don't attend lectures just to sit through dry and monotonous slide presentations. It's time to move beyond a one-way lecture format!

does not curve when exam grades average low, is extremely rude, tells people not to ask questions during the exam and then laughs in their face when they need a question reworded. i've never heard of such a tone-deaf rude prof before who just does not care about students in any way.

Rolf was actually a pretty good professor. As he's been teaching for a long time he has a routine, but is clearly passionate about the material and biology itself. He is fairly clear by what are the most important takeaways from the lecture notes (usually bolded or emphasized in some way), but the long answers on the exams can be a little vague.
-tests are fair, study the book (he asks quest from practise chapter quest) & lots of def / short answer type on midterms -approachable and helpful

You can REALLY tell he loves what he does. If you're not interested in the course material, it'll suck, but if you are into it, his lectures are FULL of interesting facts and details, which are helpful even if not directly examinable. Course was well-organised and easy to understand. Lots of info, but no trick questions.
Yawn
Great lady. A young, modern & fun. prof that tries to make the class interesting. She's patient & her exams are fair.
Good, easy course. Don't need textbook, just follow her notes and you'll do good. Basic mt's, not tricky questions or anything like that.
